scope.summary:
Goal: Implement the foundational text buffer and syntax highlighting layer for the native code editor panel. This is the root MT that all other E1 MTs build on. Expected diff shape: new Rust module `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/buffer.rs` (ropey-based text buffer with edit/query API), new module `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/highlight.rs` (tree-sitter highlight pipeline), new `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs` wiring them, and integration into the egui panel struct at `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/panel.rs`.

Exact files to create/edit:
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs` — declares buffer and highlight submodules, exports the top-level `CodeEditorPanel` egui widget struct.
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/buffer.rs` — wraps the `ropey` crate `Rope` type. Exposes: `TextBuffer::new(text: &str) -> Self`, `insert(byte_offset: usize, text: &str)`, `delete(byte_range: Range<usize>)`, `slice_to_string(line_range: Range<usize>) -> String`, `len_bytes() -> usize`, `len_lines() -> usize`, `line_to_byte(line_idx: usize) -> usize`, `byte_to_line(byte_offset: usize) -> usize`. The buffer is the sole owner of document text; all other subsystems (highlight, folding, minimap) borrow slices from it.
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/highlight.rs` — uses `tree-sitter` + language grammars (start with `tree-sitter-rust` and `tree-sitter-javascript` as bundled crates; add more via a `LanguageRegistry` map keyed on file extension). Exposes `Highlighter::new(language: Language) -> Self`, `highlight(source: &[u8]) -> Vec<HighlightSpan>` where `HighlightSpan { byte_range: Range<usize>, scope: HighlightScope }`. `HighlightScope` is an enum: `Keyword`, `String`, `Comment`, `Number`, `Function`, `Type`, `Operator`, `Other`.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/panel.rs` (create if not yet existing) — mounts a `CodeEditorPanel` egui widget: renders visible lines from the buffer, applies highlight spans as colored text runs via `egui::RichText`, provides a stable `egui::Id` for the panel, and sets AccessKit metadata.

Exact crates/functions/APIs to use:
- `ropey = "1.6"` — battle-tested rope data structure; handles large files and incremental edits cheaply.
- `tree-sitter = "0.25"` single pinned core + `tree-sitter-language` LanguageFn shim + `tree-sitter-rust = "0.24"` + a shim-compatible `tree-sitter-javascript` + `tree-sitter-highlight` [RESEARCH-CORRECTED wf_ffa74d6d 2026-06-22 from 0.22/0.21: field converged on 0.25.x core + tree-sitter-language LanguageFn shim where grammars expose LANGUAGE: LanguageFn, decoupling grammar version from core and avoiding the duplicate-core/ABI wall as more Monaco-parity languages are added; load via the shim, NOT the deprecated language() fn] — canonical grammar crates; parse incrementally using `Parser::parse_with` and the old tree for re-use.
- `egui` text rendering: `ui.painter().galley(...)` or `ui.label(egui::RichText::new(text).color(color))` per span.
- AccessKit metadata via the existing WP-011 accessibility layer (`src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/` — `mod.rs`, `registry.rs`, `snapshot.rs`, `live.rs`): register the outer panel node as `Role::TextInput` with `author_id` = `"code_editor_buffer"` through the project's accessibility registry rather than building raw AccessKit nodes directly.

React reference: `app/src/components/MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx` — the Monaco-based code block that this native surface replaces. Study the language field, readOnly flag, and status-dispatch pattern. Note that Monaco handled highlighting internally; here tree-sitter replaces it.
Backend: No direct backend call in this MT. The buffer is purely in-process. Backend code-nav APIs are wired in MT-008. Backend access is via the existing API client `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs` (reuse only; no backend rewrite).

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Add `ropey`, `tree-sitter`, `tree-sitter-rust`, `tree-sitter-javascript` to `src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml`.
2. Implement `TextBuffer` in `buffer.rs`. Keep all methods panic-free for out-of-range inputs (clamp or return `Err`).
3. Implement `Highlighter` in `highlight.rs`. Parse the full buffer on first call; on subsequent calls pass the previous tree as `old_tree` for incremental re-parse. Map tree-sitter `Highlight` indices to `HighlightScope` using a fixed lookup table against the `highlights.scm` capture names (e.g. `keyword` -> `Keyword`, `string` -> `String`).
4. Implement `CodeEditorPanel::show(&mut self, ui: &mut egui::Ui)` in `mod.rs`. For now render all lines (virtualization is MT-002). Use a `egui::ScrollArea` for vertical scroll. Each line: iterate spans overlapping the line, split text into colored runs, render with `egui::RichText`. Use the existing theme layer (`src/frontend/handshake_native/src/theme/` — `palette.rs`, `syntax.rs`, `egui_apply.rs`) for code background and syntax colors rather than hardcoded hex.
5. Wire `CodeEditorPanel` into the WP-011 docking surface by registering it through `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/pane_registry.rs` and placing it via `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/split_layout.rs` so it appears as a named pane.
6. Register AccessKit metadata through the existing accessibility layer (`src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/registry.rs`): an `author_id = "code_editor_panel"` `Role::GenericContainer` on the outer `Frame` and `author_id = "code_editor_text"` `Role::TextInput` on the inner text area so swarm agents can address the widget.

Dependencies/unblock conditions: None — this is the root MT. Requires `ropey` and `tree-sitter` crates available in the `handshake-native` crate.
Risk if missed: All other E1 MTs are blocked; no text foundation means no virtualization, no multi-cursor, no find/replace, no folding.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native` passes with tests for TextBuffer insert/delete/slice covering at least: empty buffer, single-line insert, multi-line insert, delete spanning lines, byte-to-line and line-to-byte round-trips.
AC-002: `cargo test -p handshake-native` passes with a test that parses a 10-line Rust snippet through Highlighter and returns at least one span with scope=Keyword and one with scope=Function.
AC-003: `cargo test -p handshake-native` passes with a test that parses a JS snippet and returns at least one span with scope=String.

AC-004: An egui_kittest screenshot of the CodeEditorPanel rendering a 5-line Rust snippet shows colored text (at least two distinct foreground colors present, verified by pixel sampling of the test image).
AC-005: The AccessKit tree exported by egui_kittest contains a node with author_id='code_editor_panel' and role=GenericContainer and a child node with author_id='code_editor_text' and role=TextInput.
AC-006: No `unwrap()` calls on buffer range operations in production code paths — all fallible operations return `Option` or `Result`.
proof_targets: PT-001: Run `cargo test -p handshake-native text_buffer` — all tests GREEN, output logged.
PT-002: Run `cargo test -p handshake-native highlighter` — all tests GREEN, output logged.
PT-003: Run egui_kittest screenshot test `code_editor_panel_basic` — screenshot saved to `test_output/MT-001-panel-basic.png`, pixel check passes.
PT-004: Run egui_kittest AccessKit dump test `code_editor_panel_accesskit` — JSON output contains both required author_ids.
PT-005: Run cargo tree -d from the crate dir with the enforced external artifact target and confirm a SINGLE tree-sitter core in the dependency graph.
implementation_notes: Use `ropey::Rope` not `String` from the start — retrofitting later when files are large is expensive.
tree-sitter incremental re-parse: always pass `Some(&old_tree)` to `parser.parse_with` after the first parse to get O(edit) re-parse cost.

For highlight colors, define a `fn scope_to_color(scope: HighlightScope, visuals: &egui::Visuals) -> egui::Color32` function backed by the existing theme layer (`src/frontend/handshake_native/src/theme/syntax.rs`) — keep theme-aware, not hardcoded hex.
Do not call `.to_string()` on the full rope for every frame — slice only the visible range (even before MT-002 virtualization, limit to first 1000 lines as a guard).
Wrap tree-sitter `Language` in a `Send + Sync` newtype if the egui event loop requires it.
HighlightScope must derive Clone, Debug, PartialEq for tests.

Cross-WP dependency: this MT builds on WP-011 modules (`pane_registry.rs`, `split_layout.rs`, `theme/*`, `accessibility/*`, `backend_client.rs`) already present under `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/`. These are reused, not re-created.

SALVAGE: buffer.rs + panel.rs from the prior run are high-quality and version-independent - keep/refine, do not rewrite; port highlight.rs + Cargo.toml to the 0.25.x + tree-sitter-language stack; keep the public HighlightSpan{byte_range,scope} + HighlightScope output type stable so panel.rs is unaffected.
tree-sitter safety: after porting, cargo tree -d MUST show exactly one tree-sitter core (no duplicates).
ports_from_react: app/src/components/MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx — language field, readOnly flag, status-dispatch pattern, language picker data model
app/src/lib/monaco/language_registry.ts — HANDSHAKE_CODE_LANGUAGES list: port the same language IDs to a Rust LanguageRegistry map (language_id -> tree-sitter Language)
binds_backend_api: None in this MT. Buffer is in-process only. Backend code-nav APIs are wired in MT-008 via the existing `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs` API client.

risks: RISK-001: tree-sitter grammar crates lag behind the upstream tree-sitter-rust version and may not compile against the same `tree-sitter` core version. Resolve by pinning both in lockfile and checking `cargo tree -d` for duplicates.

RISK-002: ropey's byte-offset API vs char-offset confusion causes off-by-one bugs in highlight span alignment. Mitigate by always converting highlight byte ranges via `Rope::byte_to_char` before rendering.
RISK-003: Rendering all lines without virtualization makes startup with large files (>10 k lines) block the egui frame. Guard with a 1000-line render cap until MT-002 lands.
RISK-004: AccessKit author_id collisions if multiple CodeEditorPanel instances are mounted (e.g. diff view). Use panel-instance uuid suffix appended to the author_id string.
RISK-005: tree-sitter `Language` is not `Send` in some versions — will fail if egui moves tasks to threads. Wrap in `Arc<Mutex<...>>` preemptively.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Unit test all TextBuffer range operations with both in-bounds and out-of-bounds inputs — confirm no panic.
MC-002: CI cargo test gate on the `handshake-native` crate must include MT-001 tests before any downstream MT is marked READY.
MC-003: Screenshot test must run in CI (headless wgpu or software rasterizer) and artifact the PNG.
MC-004: Cargo.lock must be committed so grammar crate versions are reproducible across machines.

corrections: panel render cap accepted for MT-001; custom virtualized egui painter deferred to MT-002
research_provenance.reconcile_after_green: pin exact working tree-sitter versions once MT-001 builds green

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_e361c5d8 (implement + fresh adversarial-review + harden)

implementation_result.proofs: AC-001..006 + PT-001..005 GREEN; lib 374 + buffer 7 + highlighter 4 + panel kittest 3; cargo tree -d single tree-sitter core; clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean; GPU pixel proof + AccessKit ancestry; PNG visually inspected by KERNEL_BUILDER = real tree-sitter highlighting.
deferred_to_later_mts: dedicated function/operator theme tokens in theme/syntax.rs - later highlight MT
multi-instance panel runtime AccessKit test - MT-009 diff view
true Tree::edit incremental reparse - later perf MT
>1000-line cap-notice text test + highlight parse->None branch test - test-hardening follow-up
implementation_result.artifact_hygiene_followup: kittest panel test writes PNG to a repo-local test_output/ dir (gitignored) in addition to the external Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test root; redirect to external-only folded into MT-002 (operator artifact directive).
